query: AI buildout after the demand signal weakens The board previously approved an accelerated AI infrastructure plan. Six months later, customer pipeline conversion is below plan, utilization of recently installed capacity is falling, and the next construction tranche is still cancellable, although technical leaders argue that slowing now could sacrifice platform leadership. Should the CFO preserve, stage, or reduce the next investment tranche, and which reversal signals should dominate the decision?
